# LLMtxt — Full Context
> Complete SDK and API documentation for deep LLM consumption

---

# llmtxt — Full Context

> Primitives and SDK for LLM agent content workflows: compression, patching, progressive disclosure, signed URLs, collaborative document lifecycle, and retrieval planning

Version: 2026.4.1
Generated: 2026-03-31T04:06:02.373Z

## Package: main

### Functions

(data: string) => Promise<Buffer>

#### decompress()

(data: Buffer) => Promise<string>

(num: number) => string

#### decodeBase62()

(str: string) => number

() => string

(data: string) => string

(text: string) => number

(originalSize: number, compressedSize: number) => number

#### createClient()

(config: LlmtxtClientConfig) => { upload(conversationId: string, content: string, options?: { format?: string; title?: string; expiresIn?: number; }): Promise<UploadResult>; fetch(signedUrl: string): Promise<FetchResult>; fetchFromConversation(slug: string, conversationId: string): Promise<FetchResult>; fetchOwned(slug: string): Promise<FetchResult>; reshare(slug: string, options?: AttachmentReshareOptions): Promise<ReshareResult>; resign(slug: string, options?: AttachmentReshareOptions): Promise<ResignResult>; createVersionPatch(original: string, updated: string): string; addVersion(slug: string, patchText: string, options?: AttachmentVersionOptions): Promise<AttachmentVersionResult>; addVersionFromContent(slug: string, original: string, updated: string, options?: AttachmentVersionOptions): Promise<AttachmentVersionResult>; isValid(signedUrl: string): boolean; }

#### getLineRange()

(content: string, start: number, end: number) => LineRangeResult

Extract a range of lines from a document, returning content and token statistics.

Line numbers are 1-based and clamped to the document boundaries. This enables agents to request only the portion of a document they need, reducing token consumption.

Parameters:
- content: The full document content.
- start: The 1-based starting line number.
- end: The 1-based ending line number (inclusive).

Returns: A `LineRangeResult` with the extracted content and token savings.

Example:
  const range = getLineRange(doc, 10, 25);
  console.log(`Saved ${range.tokensSaved} tokens`);

#### searchContent()

(content: string, query: string, contextLines?: number, maxResults?: number) => SearchResult[]

Search document content for lines matching a query string or regex.

Supports plain-text substring matching (case-insensitive) and regex patterns delimited with slashes (e.g. `/pattern/i`). Results include configurable surrounding context lines to help agents understand each match in context.

Parameters:
- content: The full document content to search.
- query: A plain-text substring or `/regex/flags` pattern.
- contextLines: Number of context lines before and after each match (default: 2).
- maxResults: Maximum number of matches to return (default: 20).

Returns: An array of `SearchResult` objects for each matching line.

Example:
  const hits = searchContent(doc, 'TODO', 3, 10);

#### detectDocumentFormat()

(content: string) => "json" | "markdown" | "code" | "text"

Detect the structural format of a document using content heuristics.

Applies the following precedence: JSON (valid `JSON.parse`), then markdown (2+ markdown signals such as headings, lists, or links), then code (2+ code signals such as `import`, `function`, or arrow syntax), and finally falls back to plain text.

Parameters:
- content: The document content to classify.

Returns: The detected format: `"json"`, `"markdown"`, `"code"`, or `"text"`.

Example:
  detectDocumentFormat('# Title\n- item'); // "markdown"

#### generateOverview()

(content: string) => DocumentOverview

Generate a structural overview of a document for progressive disclosure.

Detects the document format, splits it into logical sections, computes token counts, and extracts format-specific metadata (JSON keys or markdown table of contents). The overview allows agents to decide which sections to request in full, minimizing total token usage.

Parameters:
- content: The full document content to analyze.

Returns: A `DocumentOverview` with format, sections, and token counts.

Example:
  const overview = generateOverview(markdownDoc);
  console.log(`${overview.sections.length} sections, ${overview.tokenCount} tokens`);

#### queryJsonPath()

(content: string, path: string) => { result: unknown; tokenCount: number; path: string; }

Execute a JSONPath-style query against JSON content.

Parses the content as JSON and resolves the dot/bracket path (e.g. `$.messages[0].content` or `data.items`). Supports wildcard (`*`) for arrays and objects. Throws on invalid JSON or missing keys to provide clear error messages to calling agents.

Parameters:
- content: A valid JSON string to query.
- path: A JSONPath expression (e.g. `"$.key"`, `"items[0].name"`).

Returns: An object containing the resolved `result`, its `tokenCount`, and the original `path`.

Example:
  const { result } = queryJsonPath('{"a":{"b":42}}', '$.a.b');
  // result === 42

#### getSection()

(content: string, sectionName: string, depthAll?: boolean) => { section: Section; content: string; tokenCount: number; totalTokens: number; tokensSaved: number; } | null

Extract a named section from a document by title.

Searches the document's sections (from `generateOverview`) for a matching title using progressively looser comparisons: exact match, substring match, and alphanumeric-only substring match. When `depthAll` is `true`, child sections at deeper nesting levels are included in the extracted content.

Parameters:
- content: The full document content.
- sectionName: The section title (or substring) to search for.
- depthAll: When `true`, include all child sections nested under the match (default: `false`).

Returns: An object with the matched section, extracted content, and token savings, or `null` if not found.

Example:
  const section = getSection(doc, 'Installation', true);
  if (section) console.log(section.content);

#### extractMentions()

(content: string) => string[]

Extract  from message content.

#### extractTags()

(content: string) => string[]

Extract #tags from message content.

#### extractDirectives()

(content: string) => string[]

Extract /directives from message content.

#### buildGraph()

(messages: MessageInput[]) => KnowledgeGraph

Build a knowledge graph from an array of messages.  Nodes: agents (from fromAgentId + mentions), topics (#tags), decisions (/decision messages) Edges: mentions (agent→agent), discusses (agent→topic), decides (agent→decision), participates (agent→agent in same conversation)

#### topTopics()

(graph: KnowledgeGraph, limit?: number) => Array<{ topic: string; agents: number; }>

Find the most connected topics in the graph. Returns topics sorted by number of discussing agents.

#### topAgents()

(graph: KnowledgeGraph, limit?: number) => Array<{ agent: string; activity: number; }>

Find the most active agents in the graph. Returns agents sorted by total edge weight (mentions + discussions + decisions).

#### isPredefinedSchema()

(name: string) => name is PredefinedSchemaName

Type-guard that checks whether a string is a registered predefined schema name.

Narrows the type of `name` to `PredefinedSchemaName` when `true`, enabling safe indexing into the `predefinedSchemas` registry.

Parameters:
- name: The schema name to check.

Returns: `true` if `name` is a key in the predefined schema registry.

Example:
  if (isPredefinedSchema('prompt-v1')) {
    const schema = predefinedSchemas['prompt-v1'];
  }

#### getPredefinedSchema()

(name: string) => z.ZodSchema | undefined

Retrieve a predefined Zod schema by name.

Returns `undefined` when the name is not found in the registry, allowing callers to gracefully handle unknown schema names.

Parameters:
- name: The schema name to look up.

Returns: The matching Zod schema, or `undefined` if not found.

Example:
  const schema = getPredefinedSchema('prompt-v1');
  if (schema) schema.parse(data);

#### validateJson()

(content: unknown, schemaName?: string) => ValidationResult

Validate content as JSON, optionally against a predefined schema.

When `content` is a string it is first parsed with `JSON.parse`. The parsed value is then checked against the generic JSON schema and, when a `schemaName` is provided, against the matching predefined schema from the registry.

Parameters:
- content: The raw content to validate (string or pre-parsed value).
- schemaName: Optional name of a predefined schema to enforce.

Returns: A `ValidationResult` indicating success or listing errors.

Example:
  const result = validateJson('{"key": "value"}');
  if (result.success) console.log(result.data);

#### validateText()

(content: unknown) => ValidationResult<string>

Validate content as plain text or markdown.

Checks that the value is a string using the text format schema.

Parameters:
- content: The value to validate as text.

Returns: A `ValidationResult` with `format` set to `"text"` on success.

Example:
  const result = validateText('# Hello');

#### detectFormat()

(content: string) => "json" | "text" | "markdown"

Auto-detect whether a string is JSON, markdown, or plain text.

Attempts JSON parsing first. For non-JSON content, applies markdown heuristics (headings, lists, code blocks, links) — 2+ signals returns `"markdown"`, otherwise `"text"`. This is now consistent with the disclosure module's `detectDocumentFormat`.

Parameters:
- content: The string to inspect.

Returns: The detected format: `"json"`, `"markdown"`, or `"text"`.

Example:
  detectFormat('{"a":1}');                    // "json"
  detectFormat('# Title\n- item');            // "markdown"
  detectFormat('Hello');                      // "text"

#### validateContent()

(content: unknown, format: "json" | "text" | "markdown", schemaNameOrOptions?: string | ValidateContentOptions) => ValidationResult

Validate content for a given format, with optional schema enforcement and content size limits.

Dispatches to `validateJson` for JSON content or `validateText` for text/markdown. Enforces a byte-size limit (default 5 MB) before format validation. Returns an error result when an unsupported format string is supplied or content exceeds the limit.

Parameters:
- content: The raw content to validate.
- format: The expected content format.
- schemaNameOrOptions: A schema name string (backward compat) or options object.

Returns: A `ValidationResult` indicating success or listing errors.

Example:
  validateContent(payload, 'json', 'prompt-v1');
  validateContent(payload, 'text', { maxBytes: 10 * 1024 * 1024 });

#### autoValidate()

(content: unknown, schemaName?: string) => ValidationResult

Auto-detect the content format and then validate accordingly.

Combines `detectFormat` and `validateContent` into a single call. The content must be a string; non-string values immediately fail with an `invalid_type` error.

Parameters:
- content: The raw content to auto-detect and validate.
- schemaName: Optional predefined schema name for JSON validation.

Returns: A `ValidationResult` with the detected format and validated data.

Example:
  const result = autoValidate('{"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"hi"}]}', 'prompt-v1');

#### computeSignature()

(params: SignedUrlParams, secret: string) => string

Compute the HMAC-SHA256 signature for signed URL parameters. Delegates to the Rust WASM module.

#### computeSignatureWithLength()

(params: SignedUrlParams, secret: string, sigLength: number) => string

Compute signature with configurable length. Use 16 for short-lived URLs (default), 32 for long-lived URLs (128 bits).

#### generateSignedUrl()

(params: SignedUrlParams, config: SignedUrlConfig) => string

Generate a signed URL for accessing a document.

#### verifySignedUrl()

(url: string | URL, secret: string) => VerifyResult

Verify a signed URL's signature and expiration. Uses timing-safe comparison to prevent timing attacks.

#### computeOrgSignature()

(params: OrgSignedUrlParams, secret: string) => string

Compute the HMAC-SHA256 signature for org-scoped signed URL parameters. Includes orgId in the HMAC payload for organization-level access control. Returns 32 hex characters (128 bits) by default.

#### computeOrgSignatureWithLength()

(params: OrgSignedUrlParams, secret: string, sigLength: number) => string

Compute org-scoped signature with configurable length.

#### generateOrgSignedUrl()

(params: OrgSignedUrlParams, config: SignedUrlConfig) => string

Generate an org-scoped signed URL for accessing a document. The URL includes the org parameter for organization-level access verification.

#### verifyOrgSignedUrl()

(url: string | URL, secret: string) => VerifyResult & { orgId?: string; }

Verify an org-scoped signed URL's signature and expiration.

#### generateTimedUrl()

(params: Omit<SignedUrlParams, "expiresAt">, config: SignedUrlConfig, ttlMs?: number) => string

Generate a signed URL that expires after the given duration.

#### deriveSigningKey()

(apiKey: string) => string

Derive a per-agent signing key from their API key. Delegates to the Rust WASM module.

#### isExpired()

(expiresAt: number | null | undefined) => boolean

Check whether a timestamp has expired. Returns false for null/undefined (no expiration set).

#### compressSnapshot()

(jsonStr: string, sessionId: string, agentId: string) => Promise<CompressedSnapshot>

Compress a session snapshot JSON string. Returns compressed data + metadata for storage/retrieval.

#### decompressSnapshot()

(data: Buffer, expectedHash?: string) => Promise<string>

Decompress a snapshot back to JSON string. Verifies integrity via content hash if provided.

#### compressSessionData()

(sessionData: Record<string, unknown>, sessionId: string, agentId: string, options?: SnapshotOptions) => Promise<CompressedSnapshot>

Compress a session snapshot object directly. Serializes to JSON, then compresses.

#### decompressSessionData()

<T = Record<string, unknown>>(data: Buffer, expectedHash?: string) => Promise<T>

Decompress and parse a snapshot back to an object.

#### snapshotSummary()

(meta: SnapshotMeta) => string

Generate a human-readable summary of a snapshot for handoff messages. Useful for agents posting session summaries to ClawMsgr.

#### extractNgrams()

(text: string, n?: number) => Set<string>

Extract character-level n-grams from text. Normalizes whitespace and lowercases before extraction.

#### extractWordShingles()

(text: string, n?: number) => Set<string>

Extract word-level n-grams (shingles) from text. Better for longer content where word order matters.

#### jaccardSimilarity()

(a: Set<string>, b: Set<string>) => number

Jaccard similarity: |A ∩ B| / |A ∪ B|. Returns 0.0 (no overlap) to 1.0 (identical).

#### textSimilarity()

(a: string, b: string, ngramSize?: number) => number

Compute similarity between two texts using character n-grams. Returns 0.0 to 1.0.

#### contentSimilarity()

(a: string, b: string, shingleSize?: number) => number

Compute similarity using word shingles. Better for comparing messages or documents where word choice matters more than character patterns.

#### minHashFingerprint()

(text: string, numHashes?: number, ngramSize?: number) => number[]

Generate a compact fingerprint for content using MinHash. The fingerprint is an array of hash values that can be compared for approximate similarity without storing the full n-gram set.  Two fingerprints with many matching values indicate similar content.

#### fingerprintSimilarity()

(a: number[], b: number[]) => number

Estimate similarity between two MinHash fingerprints. Returns approximate Jaccard similarity (0.0 to 1.0).

#### rankBySimilarity()

(query: string, candidates: string[], options?: { method?: "ngram" | "shingle"; threshold?: number; }) => Array<{ index: number; score: number; }>

Rank a list of texts by similarity to a query. Returns indices sorted by descending similarity, with scores.

#### isValidTransition()

(from: DocumentState, to: DocumentState) => boolean

Check whether a state transition is allowed.

Parameters:
- from: Current document state.
- to: Target document state.

Returns: `true` if the transition is permitted by the lifecycle rules.

#### validateTransition()

(from: DocumentState, to: DocumentState) => TransitionResult

Validate a proposed state transition with a detailed result.

Parameters:
- from: Current document state.
- to: Target document state.

Returns: A result object indicating validity and allowed alternatives.

#### isEditable()

(state: DocumentState) => boolean

Check whether a document state allows content modifications.

Parameters:
- state: Current document state.

Returns: `true` if new versions can be created in this state.

#### isTerminal()

(state: DocumentState) => boolean

Check whether a document state is terminal (no further transitions).

Parameters:
- state: Current document state.

Returns: `true` if the state has no outgoing transitions.

#### reconstructVersion()

(baseContent: string, patches: VersionEntry[], targetVersion?: number) => ReconstructionResult

Reconstruct a document at a specific version by applying patches sequentially from the base content.

Parameters:
- baseContent: The original document content (version 0).
- patches: Ordered array of version entries.
- targetVersion: The version to reconstruct. Defaults to latest.

Returns: The reconstructed document content and metadata.

#### validatePatchApplies()

(content: string, patchText: string) => PatchValidationResult

Check whether a patch applies cleanly to the given content.

Parameters:
- content: The current document content.
- patchText: The unified diff to test.

Returns: Whether the patch applies and the resulting content.

#### squashPatches()

(baseContent: string, patches: VersionEntry[]) => { patchText: string; contentHash: string; tokenCount: number; }

Squash a sequence of patches into a single unified diff.  Applies all patches sequentially to the base content, then produces one diff from base to final state.

Parameters:
- baseContent: The content before the first patch.
- patches: Ordered array of version entries to squash.

Returns: A single patch text and the final content hash.

#### computeReversePatch()

(contentBefore: string, contentAfter: string) => string

Compute a reverse patch that undoes a version's changes.

Parameters:
- contentBefore: Document content before the patch was applied.
- contentAfter: Document content after the patch was applied.

Returns: A unified diff that reverts `contentAfter` back to `contentBefore`.

#### diffVersions()

(baseContent: string, patches: VersionEntry[], fromVersion: number, toVersion: number) => VersionDiffSummary

Compute a diff summary between two versions of a document.

Parameters:
- baseContent: The original document content (version 0).
- patches: Full ordered patch stack.
- fromVersion: Start version.
- toVersion: End version.

Returns: Diff statistics and patch text between the two versions.

#### evaluateApprovals()

(reviews: Review[], policy: ApprovalPolicy, currentVersion: number, now?: number) => ApprovalResult

Evaluate reviews against an approval policy.  Filters out stale and timed-out reviews, then checks whether the remaining approvals meet the policy threshold.

Parameters:
- reviews: All reviews submitted for the document.
- policy: The approval policy to evaluate against.
- currentVersion: Current document version (reviews for older versions are stale).
- now: Current timestamp (ms since epoch). Defaults to `Date.now()`.

Returns: The approval evaluation result.

#### markStaleReviews()

(reviews: Review[], currentVersion: number) => Review[]

Mark reviews as stale when a document version changes.  Returns a new array with updated review statuses. Does not mutate input.

Parameters:
- reviews: Current reviews.
- currentVersion: The new document version.

Returns: Reviews with outdated entries marked as STALE.

#### inlineRef()

(contentHash: string, originalSize: number, compressedSize: number) => ContentRef

Create a content reference for inline storage.

Parameters:
- contentHash: SHA-256 hash of the uncompressed content.
- originalSize: Size of the uncompressed content in bytes.
- compressedSize: Size of the compressed content in bytes.

Returns: An inline content reference.

#### objectStoreRef()

(storageKey: string, contentHash: string, originalSize: number, compressedSize: number) => ContentRef

Create a content reference for object-store storage.

Parameters:
- storageKey: The object key in the store.
- contentHash: SHA-256 hash of the uncompressed content.
- originalSize: Size of the uncompressed content in bytes.
- compressedSize: Size of the compressed content in bytes.

Returns: An object-store content reference.

#### versionStorageKey()

(slug: string, version: number) => string

Generate a storage key for a document version.  Convention: `attachments/{slug}/v{version}.zlib`

Parameters:
- slug: Document slug.
- version: Version number.

Returns: The object storage key.

#### shouldUseObjectStore()

(compressedSize: number, threshold?: number) => boolean

Determine whether content should be stored in object-store vs inline.  Threshold: content larger than 64KB compressed goes to object-store.

Parameters:
- compressedSize: Size of the compressed content in bytes.
- threshold: Size threshold in bytes. Defaults to 65536 (64KB).

Returns: `true` if the content should use object-store.

#### attributeVersion()

(contentBefore: string, contentAfter: string, authorId: string, entry: Pick<VersionEntry, "versionNumber" | "changelog" | "createdAt">) => VersionAttribution

Compute attribution data for a single version change.

Parameters:
- contentBefore: Document content before the change.
- contentAfter: Document content after the change.
- authorId: Agent that made the change.
- entry: The version entry metadata.

Returns: Attribution data for this version.

#### buildContributorSummary()

(attributions: VersionAttribution[]) => ContributorSummary[]

Build aggregated contributor summaries from version attributions.

Parameters:
- attributions: Array of per-version attribution data.

Returns: Contributor summaries sorted by total versions authored (descending).

#### planRetrieval()

(overview: DocumentOverview, tokenBudget: number, query?: string, options?: RetrievalOptions) => RetrievalPlan

Plan which sections to retrieve given a token budget.  When a query is provided, sections are ranked by text similarity to the query and packed greedily by descending score. Without a query, sections are packed in document order.

Parameters:
- overview: Structural overview from generateOverview().
- tokenBudget: Maximum tokens to use.
- query: Optional search query to rank sections by relevance.
- options: Planning options.

Returns: A retrieval plan with selected sections and budget accounting.

#### estimateRetrievalCost()

(overview: DocumentOverview, sectionIndices: number[]) => number

Estimate the token cost of fetching specific sections.

Parameters:
- overview: Document overview.
- sectionIndices: Indices of sections to fetch.

Returns: Total token count for the requested sections.

### Classes

#### LRUCache

typeof LRUCache

Generic least-recently-used (LRU) cache with time-to-live support.

Backed by a `Map` whose insertion order doubles as the LRU eviction order. Expired entries are lazily evicted on access. The cache tracks hit/miss statistics for observability.

Example:
  const cache = new LRUCache<string>({ maxSize: 100, ttl: 60_000 });
  cache.set('key', 'value');
  cache.get('key'); // "value"

Members:

##### cache

Map<string, CacheEntry<T>>

Internal map storing cache entries in insertion (LRU) order.

##### maxSize

number

Maximum number of entries before eviction.

##### defaultTtl

number

Default time-to-live in milliseconds for new entries.

##### stats

{ hits: number; misses: number; }

Running hit/miss counters for observability.

##### get()

(key: string) => T | undefined

Retrieve a cached value by key.

Returns `undefined` and increments the miss counter when the key is absent or its entry has expired. On a hit the entry is promoted to most-recently-used position.

Parameters:
- key: The cache key to look up.

Returns: The cached value, or `undefined` on miss or expiration.

##### set()

(key: string, value: T, ttl?: number) => void

Insert or update a cache entry.

If the cache is at capacity, the least-recently-used entry is evicted before the new entry is inserted. An existing entry with the same key is replaced (and its TTL is reset).

Parameters:
- key: The cache key.
- value: The value to cache.
- ttl: Optional per-entry TTL in milliseconds (overrides default).

##### delete()

(key: string) => void

Remove a single entry from the cache by key.

No-op if the key does not exist. Does not affect hit/miss statistics.

Parameters:
- key: The cache key to remove.

##### clear()

() => void

Remove all entries from the cache and reset hit/miss statistics.

After calling `clear()`, both the entry map and the hit/miss counters are zeroed, returning the cache to its initial state.

##### size()

() => number

Return the number of live (non-expired) entries in the cache.

Performs a lazy sweep to remove expired entries before counting.

Returns: The current number of live entries.

##### has()

(key: string) => boolean

Check whether a non-expired entry exists for the given key.

Expired entries are lazily evicted during this check. This method does not promote the entry to most-recently-used position.

Parameters:
- key: The cache key to check.

Returns: `true` if a live entry exists, `false` otherwise.

##### getStats()

() => CacheStats

Retrieve a snapshot of cache performance statistics.

Triggers a lazy sweep of expired entries (via `LRUCache.size`) so that the reported `size` reflects only live entries.

Returns: A `CacheStats` object with hits, misses, size, and hit rate.

##### resetStats()

() => void

Reset the hit/miss counters to zero without clearing cached entries.

Useful for measuring hit rate over a specific time window without evicting any cached data.

#### LlmtxtDocument

typeof LlmtxtDocument

High-level document orchestration object.  Each instance wraps a single document slug and delegates all persistence to the provided StorageAdapter. All computation (diffing, hashing, disclosure, consensus) uses the pure SDK functions.

Members:

##### slug

string

##### storage

StorageAdapter

##### getContent()

(version?: number) => Promise<string>

Get document content at a specific version (defaults to latest).

##### reconstruct()

(targetVersion?: number) => Promise<ReconstructionResult>

Reconstruct content from base + patch stack at a specific version.

##### overview()

() => Promise<DocumentOverview>

Generate structural overview of the current document.

##### section()

(name: string, depthAll?: boolean) => Promise<{ content: string; tokenCount: number; totalTokens: number; tokensSaved: number; } | null>

Extract a named section from the current document.

##### getVersions()

() => Promise<VersionEntry[]>

Get all version entries.

##### createVersion()

(newContent: string, options: CreateVersionOptions) => Promise<VersionEntry>

Create a new version from updated content.

##### diff()

(fromVersion: number, toVersion: number) => Promise<VersionDiffSummary>

Compute a diff summary between two versions.

##### squash()

() => Promise<{ patchText: string; contentHash: string; tokenCount: number; }>

Squash all patches into a single diff.

##### getState()

() => Promise<DocumentState>

Get current document state.

##### transition()

(to: DocumentState, metadata: { changedBy: string; reason?: string; }) => Promise<void>

Transition document to a new state.

##### checkApproval()

() => Promise<ApprovalResult>

Check current approval status against policy.

##### approve()

(options: { reviewerId: string; reason?: string; }) => Promise<void>

Submit an approval for the current version.

##### reject()

(options: { reviewerId: string; reason: string; }) => Promise<void>

Submit a rejection for the current version.

##### getAttributions()

() => Promise<VersionAttribution[]>

Compute attribution for all versions.

##### getContributors()

() => Promise<ContributorSummary[]>

Get aggregated contributor summaries.

##### planRetrieval()

(tokenBudget: number, query?: string, options?: RetrievalOptions) => Promise<RetrievalPlan>

Plan which sections to retrieve given a token budget.

### Interfaces

#### CacheStats

CacheStats

Snapshot of cache performance statistics.

Returned by `LRUCache.getStats`. The `hitRate` is calculated as `(hits / (hits + misses)) * 100` and rounded to two decimal places.

Members:

##### hits

number

Total number of cache hits since last reset.

##### misses

number

Total number of cache misses since last reset.

##### size

number

Current number of live (non-expired) entries in the cache.

##### maxSize

number

Maximum number of entries the cache can hold.

##### hitRate

number

Hit rate as a percentage (0-100), rounded to two decimal places.

#### LRUCacheOptions

LRUCacheOptions

Configuration options for constructing an `LRUCache` instance.

All fields are optional and have sensible defaults: 1000 entries max and a 24-hour TTL.

Members:

##### maxSize

number | undefined

Maximum number of entries before the least-recently-used entry is evicted (default: 1000).

##### ttl

number | undefined

Default time-to-live in milliseconds for new entries (default: 24 hours).

#### DiffResult

DiffResult

Members:

##### addedLines

number

##### removedLines

number

##### addedTokens

number

##### removedTokens

number

#### DocumentMeta

DocumentMeta

Metadata for a stored document.

Members:

##### id

string

##### slug

string

##### format

ContentFormat

##### contentHash

string

##### originalSize

number

##### compressedSize

number

##### tokenCount

number

##### createdAt

number

##### expiresAt

number | null

##### accessCount

number

##### lastAccessedAt

number | null

##### mode

DocumentMode | undefined

Lifecycle state (collaborative documents).

##### versionCount

number | undefined

Total number of versions.

##### currentVersion

number | undefined

Current version number.

##### storageKey

string | undefined

Object storage key when content lives in S3 instead of inline.

VersionMeta

##### changelog

##### toVersion

##### removedTokens

##### removedLines

AttachmentSharingMode

number | null | undefined

#### AttachmentVersionResult

AttachmentVersionResult

Members:

##### slug

string

##### versionNumber

number

##### patchText

string | undefined

##### contentHash

string | undefined

##### createdAt

string | undefined

##### createdBy

string | undefined

##### changelog

string | undefined

#### Section

Section

A logical section identified within a document.

Sections are detected by format-specific heuristics (headings in markdown, top-level keys in JSON, function/class declarations in code).

Members:

##### title

string

Display title of the section (heading text, JSON key, or symbol name).

##### depth

number

Nesting depth (0-based). Headings use depth = level - 1.

##### startLine

number

1-based line number where the section begins.

##### endLine

number

1-based line number where the section ends (inclusive).

##### tokenCount

number

Estimated token count for the section content.

##### type

"function" | "heading" | "json-key" | "code-block" | "class"

The structural type of the section.

#### DocumentOverview

DocumentOverview

High-level structural overview of a document.

Produced by `generateOverview`. Provides format detection, token counts, section listings, and format-specific extras (JSON keys or a markdown table of contents).

Members:

##### format

"json" | "text" | "markdown" | "code"

The detected document format.

##### lineCount

number

Total number of lines in the document.

##### tokenCount

number

Estimated total token count for the entire document.

##### sections

Section[]

Ordered list of sections found in the document.

##### keys

{ key: string; type: string; preview: string; }[] | undefined

Top-level JSON keys with type info and preview (JSON documents only).

##### toc

{ title: string; depth: number; line: number; }[] | undefined

Markdown table of contents entries (markdown documents only).

#### SearchResult

SearchResult

A single match returned by `searchContent`.

Members:

##### line

number

1-based line number of the matching line.

##### content

string

The full text of the matching line.

##### contextBefore

string[]

Lines immediately preceding the match (up to `contextLines`).

##### contextAfter

string[]

Lines immediately following the match (up to `contextLines`).

#### LineRangeResult

LineRangeResult

Result of extracting a line range from a document via `getLineRange`.

Members:

##### startLine

number

1-based line number where the extracted range begins.

##### endLine

number

1-based line number where the extracted range ends (inclusive).

##### content

string

The extracted text content for the requested line range.

##### tokenCount

number

Estimated token count for the extracted content.

##### totalLines

number

Total number of lines in the full document.

##### totalTokens

number

Estimated total token count for the full document.

##### tokensSaved

number

Number of tokens saved by extracting only this range.

#### GraphNode

GraphNode

Members:

##### id

string

##### type

"agent" | "topic" | "decision"

##### label

string

##### weight

number

#### GraphEdge

GraphEdge

Members:

##### source

string

##### target

string

##### type

"mentions" | "discusses" | "decides" | "participates"

##### weight

number

#### KnowledgeGraph

KnowledgeGraph

Members:

##### nodes

GraphNode[]

##### edges

GraphEdge[]

##### stats

{ agentCount: number; topicCount: number; decisionCount: number; edgeCount: number; }

#### MessageInput

MessageInput

Members:

##### id

string

##### fromAgentId

string

##### content

string

##### metadata

{ mentions?: string[]; directives?: string[]; tags?: string[]; } | undefined

##### createdAt

string

#### ValidationResult

ValidationResult<T>

Outcome of a content validation operation.

Returned by every validation function in this module. When `success` is `true`, `data` holds the parsed value and `format` indicates the detected content type. When `success` is `false`, `errors` describes what went wrong.

Members:

##### success

boolean

Whether the validation passed without errors.

##### data

T | undefined

The parsed/validated data, present only when `success` is `true`.

##### errors

ValidationError[] | undefined

List of validation errors, present only when `success` is `false`.

##### format

"json" | "text" | "markdown" | undefined

The detected or requested content format, when determinable.

#### ValidationError

ValidationError

A single validation error with location, message, and error code.

Modeled after Zod issue objects but flattened for easy serialization. The `path` uses dot-delimited notation (empty string for the root value).

Members:

##### path

string

Dot-delimited path to the field that failed validation (empty string for root).

##### message

string

Human-readable description of the validation failure.

##### code

string

Machine-readable error code (e.g. `"invalid_json"`, `"unknown_schema"`).

#### ValidateContentOptions

ValidateContentOptions

Options for content validation.

Members:

##### schemaName

string | undefined

Optional predefined schema name for JSON validation.

##### maxBytes

number | undefined

Maximum content size in bytes. Set to 0 to disable. Default: 5 MB.

##### maxLineBytes

number | undefined

Maximum line length in bytes. Set to 0 to disable. Default: 64 KB.

##### rejectBinary

boolean | undefined

Reject content with binary control characters (0x00-0x08). Default: true.

#### SignedUrlParams

SignedUrlParams

Parameters that uniquely identify a signed URL access grant.

Members:

##### slug

string

##### agentId

string

##### conversationId

string

##### expiresAt

number

#### SignedUrlConfig

SignedUrlConfig

Configuration for generating and verifying signed URLs.

Members:

##### secret

string

##### baseUrl

string

##### pathPrefix

string | undefined

Optional path prefix like `/attachments`. Default: root path.

##### signatureLength

number | undefined

Signature length in hex chars. Default: 16.

#### VerifyResult

VerifyResult

Outcome of verifying a signed URL.

Members:

##### valid

boolean

##### reason

"missing_params" | "expired" | "invalid_signature" | undefined

##### params

SignedUrlParams | undefined

#### OrgSignedUrlParams

OrgSignedUrlParams

Parameters for org-scoped signed URLs (Phase 5 enterprise). Extends conversation-scoped params with an organization ID.

Members:

##### orgId

string

#### SnapshotMeta

SnapshotMeta

Members:

##### sessionId

string

##### agentId

string

##### createdAt

string

##### originalSize

number

##### compressedSize

number

##### compressionRatio

number

##### tokens

number

##### contentHash

string

#### CompressedSnapshot

CompressedSnapshot

Members:

##### meta

SnapshotMeta

##### data

Buffer

#### SnapshotOptions

SnapshotOptions

Members:

##### includeDecisions

boolean | undefined

Include full decision log (default: true)

##### includeObservations

boolean | undefined

Include brain observations (default: true)

##### maxSize

number | undefined

Max content size before compression in bytes (default: 5MB)

#### StateTransition

StateTransition

Record of a lifecycle state change.

Members:

##### from

DocumentState

State before the transition.

##### to

DocumentState

State after the transition.

##### changedBy

string

Agent that initiated the transition.

##### changedAt

number

Timestamp of the transition (ms since epoch).

##### reason

string | undefined

Human-readable reason for the transition.

##### atVersion

number

Document version number at the time of transition.

#### TransitionResult

TransitionResult

Result of attempting a state transition.

Members:

##### valid

boolean

Whether the transition is allowed.

##### reason

string | undefined

Human-readable explanation when invalid.

##### allowedTargets

readonly DocumentState[]

The allowed targets from the current state (for error context).

#### VersionEntry

VersionEntry

A single version entry in a document's patch stack.

Members:

##### versionNumber

number

Sequential version number (1-based).

##### patchText

string

Unified diff patch text.

##### createdBy

string

Agent that authored this version.

##### changelog

string

One-line description of the change.

##### contentHash

string

SHA-256 hash of the resulting content after applying this patch.

##### createdAt

number

Timestamp of creation (ms since epoch).

#### ReconstructionResult

ReconstructionResult

Result of reconstructing a document at a specific version.

Members:

##### content

string

The document content at the requested version.

##### version

number

The version number that was reconstructed.

##### patchesApplied

number

Number of patches applied to reach this version.

##### contentHash

string

SHA-256 hash of the reconstructed content.

##### tokenCount

number

Token count of the reconstructed content.

#### PatchValidationResult

PatchValidationResult

Result of validating whether a patch applies cleanly.

Members:

##### applies

boolean

Whether the patch can be applied without conflicts.

##### error

string | undefined

Error message if the patch does not apply.

##### resultContent

string | undefined

The content that would result if the patch applies.

#### VersionDiffSummary

VersionDiffSummary

Summary of changes between two versions.

Members:

##### fromVersion

number

Source version number.

##### toVersion

number

Target version number.

##### addedLines

number

Lines added between versions.

##### removedLines

number

Lines removed between versions.

##### addedTokens

number

Tokens added between versions.

##### removedTokens

number

Tokens removed between versions.

##### patchText

string

Unified diff text.

#### Review

Review

A single review from an agent.

Members:

##### reviewerId

string

Agent that submitted the review.

##### status

ApprovalStatus

Current status of this review.

##### timestamp

number

Timestamp of the review action (ms since epoch).

##### reason

string | undefined

Reason or comment provided with the review.

##### atVersion

number

Version number the review applies to (`STALE` if document changed since).

#### ApprovalPolicy

ApprovalPolicy

Policy governing how approvals are evaluated.

Members:

##### requiredCount

number

Minimum number of approvals required (absolute count).  Ignored when `requiredPercentage` is set ( 0).

##### requireUnanimous

boolean

If true, all allowed reviewers must approve (overrides count/percentage).

##### allowedReviewerIds

string[]

Agent IDs allowed to review. Empty means anyone can review.

##### timeoutMs

number

Auto-expire reviews older than this (ms). 0 means no timeout.

##### requiredPercentage

number | undefined

Percentage of effective reviewers required (0-100). 0 means use requiredCount.  When  0, threshold = ceil(percentage * effectiveReviewerCount / 100).

#### ApprovalResult

ApprovalResult

Result of evaluating reviews against a policy.

Members:

##### approved

boolean

Whether the approval threshold is met.

##### approvedBy

string[]

Reviewers that have approved.

##### rejectedBy

string[]

Reviewers that have rejected.

##### pendingFrom

string[]

Reviewers that are still pending.

##### staleFrom

string[]

Reviewers whose reviews are stale (document changed since).

##### reason

string

Human-readable summary of the evaluation.

#### ContentRef

ContentRef

Reference to where a document's compressed content lives.  Inline: content is stored directly in the database (small documents). Object-store: content is stored in S3-compatible storage, referenced by key.

Members:

##### type

StorageType

Storage backend type.

##### storageKey

string | undefined

Location of the content. - For `inline`: not used (content is in the database row). - For `object-store`: the object key (e.g. `attachments/xK9mP2nQ/v3.zlib`).

##### contentHash

string

SHA-256 hash of the uncompressed content for integrity verification.

##### originalSize

number

Size of the uncompressed content in bytes.

##### compressedSize

number

Size of the compressed content in bytes.

##### compression

CompressionMethod

Compression method used.

#### StorageMetadata

StorageMetadata

Metadata about a stored document blob.

Members:

##### ref

ContentRef

Content reference.

##### createdAt

number

When the content was first stored (ms since epoch).

##### lastAccessedAt

number

When the content was last accessed (ms since epoch).

##### accessCount

number

Number of times the content has been accessed.

#### StorageAdapter

StorageAdapter

Storage adapter that platforms implement.

Members:

##### getContent()

(slug: string, version?: number) => Promise<string>

Retrieve document content at a specific version. When version is omitted, returns the latest version's content.

##### putContent()

(slug: string, version: number, content: string) => Promise<ContentRef>

Store document content for a specific version. Returns a ContentRef indicating where the content was stored.

##### getVersions()

(slug: string) => Promise<VersionEntry[]>

Get the ordered list of version entries for a document.

##### addVersion()

(slug: string, entry: VersionEntry) => Promise<void>

Append a new version entry to the document's version stack.

##### getState()

(slug: string) => Promise<DocumentState>

Get the current lifecycle state of a document.

##### setState()

(slug: string, transition: StateTransition) => Promise<void>

Record a lifecycle state transition.

##### getReviews()

(slug: string) => Promise<Review[]>

Get all reviews for a document.

##### addReview()

(slug: string, review: Review) => Promise<void>

Add or update a review for a document.

##### getApprovalPolicy()

(slug: string) => Promise<ApprovalPolicy>

Get the approval policy for a document.

#### VersionAttribution

VersionAttribution

Attribution data for a single version.

Members:

##### versionNumber

number

Version number this attribution describes.

##### authorId

string

Agent that authored the change.

##### addedLines

number

Lines added in this version.

##### removedLines

number

Lines removed in this version.

##### addedTokens

number

Tokens added in this version.

##### removedTokens

number

Tokens removed in this version.

##### sectionsModified

string[]

Section titles that were modified (detected via structural analysis).

##### changelog

string

One-line change description.

##### createdAt

number

Timestamp of the change.

#### ContributorSummary

ContributorSummary

Aggregated contribution summary for a single agent.

Members:

##### agentId

string

Agent identifier.

##### versionsAuthored

number

Number of versions this agent authored.

##### totalTokensAdded

number

Total tokens added across all versions.

##### totalTokensRemoved

number

Total tokens removed across all versions.

##### netTokens

number

Net token impact (added - removed).

##### firstContribution

number

Timestamp of first contribution.

##### lastContribution

number

Timestamp of most recent contribution.

##### sectionsModified

string[]

Unique section titles this agent modified.

#### PlannedSection

PlannedSection

A section selected for retrieval.

Members:

##### sectionIndex

number

Index into the overview's sections array.

##### title

string

Section title.

##### tokenCount

number

Estimated token cost for this section.

##### reason

"query-match" | "structural" | "budget-fit"

Why this section was selected.

##### score

number | undefined

Relevance score (0-1) when selected by query match.

#### RetrievalPlan

RetrievalPlan

The output of retrieval planning.

Members:

##### sections

PlannedSection[]

Sections selected for retrieval, ordered by relevance.

##### totalTokens

number

Total tokens across all selected sections.

##### budgetRemaining

number

Remaining token budget after selection.

##### tokensSaved

number

Tokens saved compared to fetching the full document.

##### fullDocumentFits

boolean

Whether the full document fits within budget (no planning needed).

#### RetrievalOptions

RetrievalOptions

Options for retrieval planning.

Members:

##### minScore

number | undefined

Minimum similarity score to include a section (0-1). Default: 0.1

##### includeIntro

boolean | undefined

Always include the first section (typically title/intro). Default: true

#### CreateVersionOptions

CreateVersionOptions

Options for creating a new version.

Members:

##### agentId

string

Agent creating this version.

##### changelog

string

One-line change description.

#### LlmtxtDocumentOptions

LlmtxtDocumentOptions

Options for constructing an LlmtxtDocument.

Members:

##### slug

string

Document slug.

##### storage

StorageAdapter

Storage adapter for persistence.

### Types

#### ContentFormat

ContentFormat

Supported content formats.

#### DocumentMode

DocumentMode

Lifecycle state for collaborative documents.

#### AttachmentAccessMode

AttachmentAccessMode

Attachment fetch mode supported by the bridge/API layer.

#### AttachmentSharingMode

AttachmentSharingMode

Share state persisted by the API layer for an attachment.

#### ResignResult

ReshareResult

Backward-compatible alias. Prefer `ReshareResult`.

#### PredefinedSchemaName

"prompt-v1"

Union of all registered predefined schema name strings.

Derived from the keys of the `predefinedSchemas` registry. Currently resolves to `"prompt-v1"`.

#### JsonFormat

string | number | boolean | unknown[] | Record<string, unknown> | null

Inferred TypeScript type for any valid JSON value (object, array, primitive).

Derived from `jsonFormatSchema`. Represents the union of all JSON-compatible types: `Record<string, unknown>`, `unknown[]`, `string`, `number`, `boolean`, and `null`.

#### TextFormat

string

Inferred TypeScript type for plain text content.

Derived from `textFormatSchema`. Resolves to `string`.

#### MarkdownFormat

string

Inferred TypeScript type for markdown content (string alias).

Derived from `markdownFormatSchema`. Resolves to `string`.

#### PromptV1

{ messages: { role: "system" | "user" | "assistant"; content: string; }[]; system?: string | undefined; temperature?: number | undefined; max_tokens?: number | undefined; }

Inferred TypeScript type for the standard LLM prompt format.

Derived from `promptV1Schema`. Includes optional `system` string, required `messages` array, and optional `temperature` and `max_tokens`.

#### PromptMessage

{ role: "system" | "user" | "assistant"; content: string; }

Inferred TypeScript type for a single prompt message with role and content.

Derived from `promptMessageSchema`. Each message has a `role` (`"system"`, `"user"`, or `"assistant"`) and a `content` string.

#### CompressRequest

{ content: string; format: "json" | "text" | "markdown"; schema?: string | undefined; metadata?: Record<string, unknown> | undefined; }

Inferred TypeScript type for content compression request payloads.

Derived from `compressRequestSchema`. Includes required `content`, optional `format`, optional `schema`, and optional `metadata`.

#### DecompressRequest

{ slug: string; }

Inferred TypeScript type for content decompression request payloads.

Derived from `decompressRequestSchema`. Contains a required `slug` field.

#### SearchRequest

{ query: string; slugs: string[]; }

Inferred TypeScript type for content search request payloads.

Derived from `searchRequestSchema`. Contains a required `query` string and a non-empty `slugs` array.

#### DocumentState

DocumentState

Lifecycle state of a collaborative document.

#### ApprovalStatus

ApprovalStatus

Status of an individual review.

#### StorageType

StorageType

How document content is stored.

#### CompressionMethod

CompressionMethod

Compression method used for stored content.

### Variables

#### jsonFormatSchema

z.ZodUnion<[z.ZodRecord<z.ZodString, z.ZodUnknown>, z.ZodArray<z.ZodUnknown, "many">, z.ZodString, z.ZodNumber, z.ZodBoolean, z.ZodNull]>

Schema that accepts any valid JSON value.

Matches objects, arrays, strings, numbers, booleans, and null. Used as the baseline structural check before optional predefined-schema enforcement.

#### textFormatSchema

z.ZodString

Schema that accepts any string value (plain text or markdown).

No structural constraints beyond being a string. Used by `validateText` in the validation layer.

#### markdownFormatSchema

z.ZodString

Schema for markdown content, stored as a plain string.

Functionally identical to `textFormatSchema`. Exists as a distinct export so callers can signal intent when working with markdown-specific pipelines.

#### promptMessageSchema

z.ZodObject<{ role: z.ZodEnum<["system", "user", "assistant"]>; content: z.ZodString; }, "strip", z.ZodTypeAny, { role: "system" | "user" | "assistant"; content: string; }, { role: "system" | "user" | "assistant"; content: string; }>

Schema for a single message within an LLM prompt conversation.

Validates that each message has a `role` (system, user, or assistant) and a `content` string, matching the format used by OpenAI and Anthropic APIs.

#### promptV1Schema

z.ZodObject<{ system: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodString>; messages: z.ZodArray<z.ZodObject<{ role: z.ZodEnum<["system", "user", "assistant"]>; content: z.ZodString; }, "strip", z.ZodTypeAny, { role: "system" | "user" | "assistant"; content: string; }, { role: "system" | "user" | "assistant"; content: string; }>, "many">; temperature: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodNumber>; max_tokens: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodNumber>; }, "strip", z.ZodTypeAny, { messages: { role: "system" | "user" | "assistant"; content: string; }[]; system?: string | undefined; temperature?: number | undefined; max_tokens?: number | undefined; }, { messages: { role: "system" | "user" | "assistant"; content: string; }[]; system?: string | undefined; temperature?: number | undefined; max_tokens?: number | undefined; }>

Schema for the standard LLM prompt format (OpenAI / Anthropic style).

Validates an object with an optional `system` prompt, a non-empty `messages` array of `promptMessageSchema` entries, and optional `temperature` (0-2) and `max_tokens` (positive integer) fields.

#### predefinedSchemas

{ readonly 'prompt-v1': z.ZodObject<{ system: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodString>; messages: z.ZodArray<z.ZodObject<{ role: z.ZodEnum<["system", "user", "assistant"]>; content: z.ZodString; }, "strip", z.ZodTypeAny, { role: "system" | "user" | "assistant"; content: string; }, { role: "system" | "user" | "assistant"; content: string; }>, "many">; temperature: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodNumber>; max_tokens: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodNumber>; }, "strip", z.ZodTypeAny, { messages: { role: "system" | "user" | "assistant"; content: string; }[]; system?: string | undefined; temperature?: number | undefined; max_tokens?: number | undefined; }, { messages: { role: "system" | "user" | "assistant"; content: string; }[]; system?: string | undefined; temperature?: number | undefined; max_tokens?: number | undefined; }>; }

Registry of predefined content schemas keyed by name.

Used by the validation layer to look up schemas by string name at runtime. Extend this object to register additional predefined formats.

#### compressRequestSchema

z.ZodObject<{ content: z.ZodString; format: z.ZodDefault<z.ZodOptional<z.ZodEnum<["json", "text", "markdown"]>>>; schema: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodString>; metadata: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodRecord<z.ZodString, z.ZodUnknown>>; }, "strip", z.ZodTypeAny, { content: string; format: "json" | "text" | "markdown"; schema?: string | undefined; metadata?: Record<string, unknown> | undefined; }, { content: string; format?: "json" | "text" | "markdown" | undefined; schema?: string | undefined; metadata?: Record<string, unknown> | undefined; }>

Schema for incoming content compression requests.

Validates that the request includes non-empty `content` and an optional `format` (defaults to `"text"`), optional `schema` name for predefined validation, and optional arbitrary `metadata`.

#### decompressRequestSchema

z.ZodObject<{ slug: z.ZodString; }, "strip", z.ZodTypeAny, { slug: string; }, { slug: string; }>

Schema for incoming content decompression requests.

Validates that the request includes a non-empty `slug` identifying the stored document to decompress.

#### searchRequestSchema

z.ZodObject<{ query: z.ZodString; slugs: z.ZodArray<z.ZodString, "many">; }, "strip", z.ZodTypeAny, { query: string; slugs: string[]; }, { query: string; slugs: string[]; }>

Schema for incoming content search requests.

Validates that the request includes a non-empty `query` string and at least one document `slug` to search within.

#### DEFAULT_MAX_CONTENT_BYTES

number

Default maximum content size in bytes (5 MB).

#### DEFAULT_MAX_LINE_BYTES

number

Default maximum line length in bytes (64 KB).

#### DOCUMENT_STATES

readonly DocumentState[]

All valid document states in lifecycle order.

#### DEFAULT_APPROVAL_POLICY

ApprovalPolicy

Default approval policy: 1 approval, no timeout.

---

# @llmtxt/backend — Full Context

Version: 1.0.0
Generated: 2026-03-31T17:01:14.526Z

## Package: main

### Functions

#### getDocumentCacheKey()

(slug: string, type?: "content" | "metadata") => string

Generate cache key for a document

#### shouldSkipCache()

(request: FastifyRequest) => boolean

Check if cache should be skipped based on query params

#### cacheDocumentContent()

(request: FastifyRequest<{ Params: { slug: string; }; Querystring: Record<string, string>; }>, reply: FastifyReply) => Promise<void>

Middleware to cache document content Usage: app.get('/documents/:slug', cacheDocumentContent, async (request, reply) =  ... )

#### cacheDocumentMetadata()

(request: FastifyRequest<{ Params: { slug: string; }; Querystring: Record<string, string>; }>, reply: FastifyReply) => Promise<void>

Middleware to cache document metadata

#### setCachedContent()

(slug: string, content: string, ttl?: number) => void

Store document content in cache

#### setCachedMetadata()

(slug: string, metadata: Record<string, unknown>, ttl?: number) => void

Store document metadata in cache

#### invalidateDocumentCache()

(slug: string) => void

Invalidate cache for a document

#### invalidateAllCache()

() => void

Invalidate all cache

#### getCacheStats()

() => { content: ReturnType<typeof contentCache.getStats>; metadata: ReturnType<typeof metadataCache.getStats>; }

Get cache stats for both caches

#### apiRoutes()

(fastify: FastifyInstance) => Promise<void>

Register core document API routes: compress, decompress, validate, search, schemas, and cache management.

#### disclosureRoutes()

(fastify: FastifyInstance) => Promise<void>

Register progressive disclosure routes: overview, sections, toc, search, lines, raw, query, and batch endpoints for token-efficient content retrieval.

#### versionRoutes()

(fastify: FastifyInstance) => Promise<void>

Register version management routes: document update, version listing, version retrieval, and pairwise diff computation.

#### authRoutes()

(fastify: FastifyInstance) => Promise<void>

Register authentication routes by proxying all /auth/* requests to the better-auth handler.

#### requireAuth()

(request: FastifyRequest, reply: FastifyReply) => Promise<undefined>

Authenticate the request via session cookie. Populates request.user and request.session, or returns 401.

#### requireRegistered()

(request: FastifyRequest, reply: FastifyReply) => Promise<undefined>

Require an authenticated, non-anonymous user. Calls requireAuth first, then rejects anonymous sessions with 403.

#### requireOwner()

(request: FastifyRequest, reply: FastifyReply) => Promise<undefined>

Require the authenticated user to be the document owner. Checks slug from route params against document ownerId. Returns 403 if not owner, 404 if document not found.

#### lifecycleRoutes()

(fastify: FastifyInstance) => Promise<void>

Register lifecycle and consensus routes: state transitions, approve/reject voting, approval listing, and contributor attribution.

#### patchRoutes()

(fastify: FastifyInstance) => Promise<void>

Register patch route: POST /documents/:slug/patch to apply a unified diff and create a new version. Requires authentication and editable document state.

#### similarityRoutes()

(fastify: FastifyInstance) => Promise<void>

Register similarity route: GET /documents/:slug/similar?q=query&method=ngram&threshold=0 to rank document sections by similarity to a query.

#### graphRoutes()

(fastify: FastifyInstance) => Promise<void>

Register knowledge graph route: GET /documents/:slug/graph to extract mentions, #tags, and /directives from document content.

#### retrievalRoutes()

(fastify: FastifyInstance) => Promise<void>

Register retrieval planning route: POST /documents/:slug/plan-retrieval for token-budget-aware section selection. Ranks sections by relevance and greedily packs within a token budget.

#### signedUrlRoutes()

(fastify: FastifyInstance) => Promise<void>

Register signed URL route: POST /signed-urls to generate time-limited HMAC-signed access tokens for document retrieval. Requires owner authentication.

#### getDocumentWithContent()

(slug: string, request: FastifyRequest) => Promise<{ content: string; id: string; slug: string; format: string; contentHash: string; compressedData: unknown; originalSize: number; compressedSize: number; tokenCount: number | null; createdAt: number; expiresAt: number | null; accessCount: number; lastAccessedAt: number | null; state: string; ownerId: string | null; isAnonymous: boolean; storageType: string; storageKey: string | null; currentVersion: number; versionCount: number; sharingMode: string; approvalRequiredCount: number; approvalRequireUnanimous: boolean; approvalAllowedReviewers: string; approvalTimeoutMs: number; } | null>

Fetch a document by slug, resolving content from cache or database with decompression.

#### handleContentNegotiation()

(request: FastifyRequest, reply: FastifyReply, slug: string) => Promise<boolean>

Handle content negotiation for slug requests. Returns true if the request was handled (response sent), false otherwise.

#### extractSlugWithExtension()

(urlPath: string) => { slug: string; ext: string; } | null

Check if a URL path looks like a document slug with an explicit extension. Supported extensions: .txt, .json, .md

#### extractSlug()

(urlPath: string) => string | null

Check if a URL path looks like a document slug. Slugs are short alphanumeric strings at the root level. Returns the slug if valid, null otherwise.

#### renderViewHtml()

(slug: string, data: any) => string

Render the server-side HTML view template for a document page, including meta tags and structured content display.

### Types

#### User

{ id: string; name: string; email: string; emailVerified: boolean; image: string | null; createdAt: Date; updatedAt: Date; isAnonymous: boolean | null; agentId: string | null; expiresAt: number | null; }

#### NewUser

{ id: string; email: string; createdAt: Date; updatedAt: Date; name?: string | undefined; emailVerified?: boolean | undefined; image?: string | null | undefined; isAnonymous?: boolean | null | undefined; agentId?: string | null | undefined; expiresAt?: number | null | undefined; }

#### Session

{ id: string; createdAt: Date; updatedAt: Date; expiresAt: Date; userId: string; token: string; ipAddress: string | null; userAgent: string | null; }

#### NewSession

{ id: string; createdAt: Date; updatedAt: Date; expiresAt: Date; userId: string; token: string; ipAddress?: string | null | undefined; userAgent?: string | null | undefined; }

#### Document

{ id: string; createdAt: number; isAnonymous: boolean; expiresAt: number | null; slug: string; format: string; contentHash: string; compressedData: unknown; originalSize: number; compressedSize: number; tokenCount: number | null; accessCount: number; lastAccessedAt: number | null; state: string; ownerId: string | null; storageType: string; storageKey: string | null; currentVersion: number; versionCount: number; sharingMode: string; approvalRequiredCount: number; approvalRequireUnanimous: boolean; approvalAllowedReviewers: string; approvalTimeoutMs: number; }

#### NewDocument

{ id: string; createdAt: number; slug: string; format: string; contentHash: string; originalSize: number; compressedSize: number; isAnonymous?: boolean | undefined; expiresAt?: number | null | undefined; compressedData?: unknown; tokenCount?: number | null | undefined; accessCount?: number | undefined; lastAccessedAt?: number | null | undefined; state?: string | undefined; ownerId?: string | null | undefined; storageType?: string | undefined; storageKey?: string | null | undefined; currentVersion?: number | undefined; versionCount?: number | undefined; sharingMode?: string | undefined; approvalRequiredCount?: number | undefined; approvalRequireUnanimous?: boolean | undefined; approvalAllowedReviewers?: string | undefined; approvalTimeoutMs?: number | undefined; }

#### Version

{ id: string; createdAt: number; contentHash: string; compressedData: unknown; tokenCount: number | null; storageType: string; storageKey: string | null; documentId: string; versionNumber: number; createdBy: string | null; changelog: string | null; patchText: string | null; baseVersion: number | null; }

#### NewVersion

{ id: string; createdAt: number; contentHash: string; documentId: string; versionNumber: number; compressedData?: unknown; tokenCount?: number | null | undefined; storageType?: string | undefined; storageKey?: string | null | undefined; createdBy?: string | null | undefined; changelog?: string | null | undefined; patchText?: string | null | undefined; baseVersion?: number | null | undefined; }

#### StateTransition

{ id: string; documentId: string; fromState: string; toState: string; changedBy: string; changedAt: number; reason: string | null; atVersion: number; }

#### NewStateTransition

{ id: string; documentId: string; fromState: string; toState: string; changedBy: string; changedAt: number; atVersion: number; reason?: string | null | undefined; }

#### Approval

{ timestamp: number; id: string; documentId: string; reason: string | null; atVersion: number; reviewerId: string; status: string; }

#### NewApproval

{ timestamp: number; id: string; documentId: string; atVersion: number; reviewerId: string; status: string; reason?: string | null | undefined; }

#### Contributor

{ id: string; agentId: string; documentId: string; versionsAuthored: number; totalTokensAdded: number; totalTokensRemoved: number; netTokens: number; firstContribution: number; lastContribution: number; sectionsModified: string; displayName: string | null; }

#### NewContributor

{ id: string; agentId: string; documentId: string; firstContribution: number; lastContribution: number; versionsAuthored?: number | undefined; totalTokensAdded?: number | undefined; totalTokensRemoved?: number | undefined; netTokens?: number | undefined; sectionsModified?: string | undefined; displayName?: string | null | undefined; }

#### SignedUrlToken

{ id: string; createdAt: number; agentId: string; expiresAt: number; slug: string; accessCount: number; lastAccessedAt: number | null; documentId: string; conversationId: string; orgId: string | null; signature: string; signatureLength: number; revoked: boolean; }

#### NewSignedUrlToken

{ id: string; createdAt: number; agentId: string; expiresAt: number; slug: string; documentId: string; conversationId: string; signature: string; accessCount?: number | undefined; lastAccessedAt?: number | null | undefined; orgId?: string | null | undefined; signatureLength?: number | undefined; revoked?: boolean | undefined; }

#### VersionAttribution

{ id: string; createdAt: number; documentId: string; versionNumber: number; changelog: string; sectionsModified: string; authorId: string; addedLines: number; removedLines: number; addedTokens: number; removedTokens: number; }

#### NewVersionAttribution

{ id: string; createdAt: number; documentId: string; versionNumber: number; authorId: string; changelog?: string | undefined; sectionsModified?: string | undefined; addedLines?: number | undefined; removedLines?: number | undefined; addedTokens?: number | undefined; removedTokens?: number | undefined; }

#### InsertUser

{ id: string; email: string; createdAt: Date; updatedAt: Date; name?: string | undefined; emailVerified?: boolean | undefined; image?: string | null | undefined; isAnonymous?: boolean | null | undefined; agentId?: string | null | undefined; expiresAt?: number | null | undefined; }

#### SelectUser

{ id: string; name: string; email: string; emailVerified: boolean; image: string | null; createdAt: Date; updatedAt: Date; isAnonymous: boolean | null; agentId: string | null; expiresAt: number | null; }

#### InsertSession

{ id: string; createdAt: Date; updatedAt: Date; expiresAt: Date; userId: string; token: string; ipAddress?: string | null | undefined; userAgent?: string | null | undefined; }

#### SelectSession

{ id: string; createdAt: Date; updatedAt: Date; expiresAt: Date; userId: string; token: string; ipAddress: string | null; userAgent: string | null; }

#### InsertDocument

{ id: string; createdAt: number; slug: string; format: string; contentHash: string; originalSize: number; compressedSize: number; isAnonymous?: boolean | undefined; expiresAt?: number | null | undefined; compressedData?: any; tokenCount?: number | null | undefined; accessCount?: number | undefined; lastAccessedAt?: number | null | undefined; state?: string | undefined; ownerId?: string | null | undefined; storageType?: string | undefined; storageKey?: string | null | undefined; currentVersion?: number | undefined; versionCount?: number | undefined; sharingMode?: string | undefined; approvalRequiredCount?: number | undefined; approvalRequireUnanimous?: boolean | undefined; approvalAllowedReviewers?: string | undefined; approvalTimeoutMs?: number | undefined; }

#### SelectDocument

{ id: string; createdAt: number; isAnonymous: boolean; expiresAt: number | null; slug: string; format: string; contentHash: string; originalSize: number; compressedSize: number; tokenCount: number | null; accessCount: number; lastAccessedAt: number | null; state: string; ownerId: string | null; storageType: string; storageKey: string | null; currentVersion: number; versionCount: number; sharingMode: string; approvalRequiredCount: number; approvalRequireUnanimous: boolean; approvalAllowedReviewers: string; approvalTimeoutMs: number; compressedData?: any; }

#### InsertVersion

{ id: string; createdAt: number; contentHash: string; documentId: string; versionNumber: number; compressedData?: any; tokenCount?: number | null | undefined; storageType?: string | undefined; storageKey?: string | null | undefined; createdBy?: string | null | undefined; changelog?: string | null | undefined; patchText?: string | null | undefined; baseVersion?: number | null | undefined; }

#### SelectVersion

{ id: string; createdAt: number; contentHash: string; tokenCount: number | null; storageType: string; storageKey: string | null; documentId: string; versionNumber: number; createdBy: string | null; changelog: string | null; patchText: string | null; baseVersion: number | null; compressedData?: any; }

#### InsertStateTransition

{ id: string; documentId: string; fromState: string; toState: string; changedBy: string; changedAt: number; atVersion: number; reason?: string | null | undefined; }

#### SelectStateTransition

{ id: string; documentId: string; fromState: string; toState: string; changedBy: string; changedAt: number; reason: string | null; atVersion: number; }

#### InsertApproval

{ timestamp: number; id: string; documentId: string; atVersion: number; reviewerId: string; status: string; reason?: string | null | undefined; }

#### SelectApproval

{ timestamp: number; id: string; documentId: string; reason: string | null; atVersion: number; reviewerId: string; status: string; }

#### InsertContributor

{ id: string; agentId: string; documentId: string; firstContribution: number; lastContribution: number; versionsAuthored?: number | undefined; totalTokensAdded?: number | undefined; totalTokensRemoved?: number | undefined; netTokens?: number | undefined; sectionsModified?: string | undefined; displayName?: string | null | undefined; }

#### SelectContributor

{ id: string; agentId: string; documentId: string; versionsAuthored: number; totalTokensAdded: number; totalTokensRemoved: number; netTokens: number; firstContribution: number; lastContribution: number; sectionsModified: string; displayName: string | null; }

#### InsertSignedUrlToken

{ id: string; createdAt: number; agentId: string; expiresAt: number; slug: string; documentId: string; conversationId: string; signature: string; accessCount?: number | undefined; lastAccessedAt?: number | null | undefined; orgId?: string | null | undefined; signatureLength?: number | undefined; revoked?: boolean | undefined; }

#### SelectSignedUrlToken

{ id: string; createdAt: number; agentId: string; expiresAt: number; slug: string; accessCount: number; lastAccessedAt: number | null; documentId: string; conversationId: string; orgId: string | null; signature: string; signatureLength: number; revoked: boolean; }

#### InsertVersionAttribution

{ id: string; createdAt: number; documentId: string; versionNumber: number; authorId: string; changelog?: string | undefined; sectionsModified?: string | undefined; addedLines?: number | undefined; removedLines?: number | undefined; addedTokens?: number | undefined; removedTokens?: number | undefined; }

#### SelectVersionAttribution

{ id: string; createdAt: number; documentId: string; versionNumber: number; changelog: string; sectionsModified: string; authorId: string; addedLines: number; removedLines: number; addedTokens: number; removedTokens: number; }

### Variables

#### users

baseColumn: never; identity: undefined; generated: undefined; }, {}>; expiresAt: import("drizzle-orm/sqlite-core").SQLiteColumn<{ name: string; tableName: "users"; dataType: "number int53"; data: number; driverParam: number; notNull: false; hasDefault: false; isPrimaryKey: false; isAutoincrement: false; hasRuntimeDefault: false; enumValues: undefined; baseColumn: never; identity: undefined; generated: undefined; }, {}>; }; dialect: "sqlite"; }>

Users table - supports both anonymous (24hr TTL) and registered accounts.  Anonymous users get a generated ID and no credentials. They are auto-purged after `expiresAt`. Registered users provide email/password and persist indefinitely until explicitly deleted.

#### sessions

import("drizzle-orm/sqlite-core").SQLiteTableWithColumns<{ name: "sessions"; schema: undefined; columns: { id: import("drizzle-orm/sqlite-core").SQLiteColumn<{ name: string; tableName: "sessions"; dataType: "string"; data: string; driverParam: string; notNull: true; hasDefault: false; isPrimaryKey: true; isAutoincrement: false; hasRuntimeDefault: false; enumValues: [string, ...string[]]; baseColumn: never; identity: undefined; isAutoincrement: false; hasRuntimeDefault: false; enumValues: undefined; baseColumn: never; identity: undefined; generated: undefined; }, {}>; }; dialect: "sqlite"; }>

Sessions table - server-side session tokens for both user types.  Anonymous users get a session on first document creation. Registered users get a session on login. Sessions are invalidated on logout or expiration.

#### accounts

Accounts table — better-auth manages OAuth and credential providers.

#### verifications

import("drizzle-orm/sqlite-core").SQLiteTableWithColumns<{ name: "verifications"; schema: undefined; columns: { id: import("drizzle-orm/sqlite-core").SQLiteColumn<{ name: string; tableName: "verifications"; driverParam: number; notNull: false; hasDefault: false; isPrimaryKey: false; isAutoincrement: false; hasRuntimeDefault: false; enumValues: undefined; baseColumn: never; identity: undefined; generated: undefined; }, {}>; updatedAt: import("drizzle-orm/sqlite-core").SQLiteColumn<{ name: string; tableName: "verifications"; dataType: "object date"; data: Date; driverParam: number; notNull: false; hasDefault: false; isPrimaryKey: false; isAutoincrement: false; hasRuntimeDefault: false; enumValues: undefined; baseColumn: never; identity: undefined; generated: undefined; }, {}>; }; dialect: "sqlite"; }>

Verifications table — better-auth email verification and password reset tokens.

Approvals table - stores individual review/approval records.  Maps directly to the SDK Review interface from consensus.ts. Each row is one review action; the latest per reviewer wins.

Contributors table - materialized aggregation of per-agent attribution.  Maps directly to the SDK ContributorSummary interface. Denormalized from version + attribution data for fast reads. Refreshed on each version creation via application logic.

#### signedUrlTokens

data: number; driverParam: number; notNull: true; hasDefault: true; isPrimaryKey: false; isAutoincrement: false; hasRuntimeDefault: false; enumValues: undefined; baseColumn: never; identity: undefined; generated: undefined; }, {}>; expiresAt: import("drizzle-orm/sqlite-core").SQLiteColumn<{ name: string; tableName: "signed_url_tokens"; dataType: "number int53"; data: number; driverParam: number; notNull: true; hasDefault: false; isPrimaryKey: false; isAutoincrement: false; hasRuntimeDefault: false; enumValues: undefined; baseColumn: never; identity: undefined; generated: undefined; }, {}>; createdAt: import("drizzle-orm/sqlite-core").SQLiteColumn<{ name: string; tableName: "signed_url_tokens"; dataType: "number int53"; data: number; driverParam: number; notNull: true; hasDefault: false; isPrimaryKey: false; isAutoincrement: false; hasRuntimeDefault: false; enumValues: undefined; baseColumn: never; identity: undefined; generated: undefined; }, {}>; revoked: import("drizzle-orm/sqlite-core").SQLiteColumn<{ name: string; tableName: "signed_url_tokens"; dataType: "boolean"; data: boolean; driverParam: number; notNull: true; hasDefault: true; isPrimaryKey: false; isAutoincrement: false; hasRuntimeDefault: false; enumValues: undefined; baseColumn: never; identity: undefined; generated: undefined; }, {}>; accessCount: import("drizzle-orm/sqlite-core").SQLiteColumn<{ name: string; tableName: "signed_url_tokens"; dataType: "number int53"; data: number; driverParam: number; notNull: true; hasDefault: true; isPrimaryKey: false; isAutoincrement: false; hasRuntimeDefault: false; enumValues: undefined; baseColumn: never; identity: undefined; generated: undefined; }, {}>; lastAccessedAt: import("drizzle-orm/sqlite-core").SQLiteColumn<{ name: string; tableName: "signed_url_tokens"; dataType: "number int53"; data: number; driverParam: number; notNull: false; hasDefault: false; isPrimaryKey: false; isAutoincrement: false; hasRuntimeDefault: false; enumValues: undefined; baseColumn: never; identity: undefined; generated: undefined; }, {}>; }; dialect: "sqlite"; }>

Signed URL tokens table - persists generated signed URL grants.  Maps to the SDK SignedUrlParams interface. Each row represents an active access grant. Expired tokens are cleaned up by the purge job. Supports both conversation-scoped and org-scoped signatures.

#### versionAttributions

tableName: "version_attributions"; dataType: "number int53"; data: number; driverParam: number; notNull: true; hasDefault: false; isPrimaryKey: false; isAutoincrement: false; hasRuntimeDefault: false; enumValues: undefined; baseColumn: never; identity: undefined; generated: undefined; }, {}>; }; dialect: "sqlite"; }>

Version attributions table - per-version diff metadata for attribution.  Maps directly to the SDK VersionAttribution interface. Stores the computed diff stats (lines/tokens added/removed, sections modified) for each version, enabling fast attribution queries without recomputing diffs.
